findings.evidence: Nearby APIs expose the scalar coefficient: `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_tmul_single` has `(g : G) (r : k)` in `code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/DiagonalizableGroupBaseChange.lean:113`, and `RootsOfUnityGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_tmul_single_generator` has `(r : k)` in `code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/RootsOfUnityBaseChange.lean:92`; Mathlib has `LaurentPolynomial.single_eq_C_mul_T` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Polynomial/Laurent.lean:181`.
findings.fix: State the primary lemma with an extra `(r : k)`, e.g. on `s ⊗ₜ[k] (LaurentPolynomial.C r * LaurentPolynomial.T n)` (or `AddMonoidAlgebra.single n r`), giving `s • (r • ((u ^ n : Aˣ) : A))`, and derive the current `T` lemma as the `r = 1` specialization.
findings.issue: `baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_tmul_T` fixes the Laurent coefficient to `1`, so users cannot rewrite the natural pure tensors `s ⊗ C r * T n` / `single n r` directly.

findings.evidence: The theorem evaluates only `(s ⊗ₜ[k] LaurentPolynomial.T n)` at lines 131-134, while the base inverse formula should match the coefficient-bearing form above.
findings.fix: Generalize it in parallel to the coefficient-bearing Laurent monomial form, and recover the existing statement by specializing `r = 1`.
findings.issue: `mapValue_baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_tmul_T` repeats the same coefficient-one specialization for the naturality evaluation lemma.

summary: The main equivalence is at the expected level, but the inverse evaluation API is under-generalized compared with the existing monoid-algebra base-change APIs: it only exposes coefficient-one Laurent monomials.
